evals.prompt: I want to build a meditation and breathwork app in React Native. Think Calm meets Headspace but more personal — warm, organic feel, not clinical. It needs a timer screen with animated breathing circle, a library of sessions you can browse, and a progress/stats dashboard. Expo is fine. I care a lot about how it feels — the animations, the transitions, the whole vibe. Make it feel like a luxury product, not a generic health app.
evals.expected_output: Should assess app personality (smooth animation feel, warm color temperature, spacious density, display font with character), choose a warm non-blue primary color, define spring presets, recommend specific Reanimated spring config for the breathing animation, use semantic dark mode tokens, and produce code that feels intentional rather than generic blue/gray/Inter.
assertions: Announces it is using the react-native-mobile skill
Assesses animation personality and explicitly chooses smooth or similar (not snappy)
Chooses a warm primary color — NOT blue-500 or any generic blue
Recommends or uses a display font that is NOT Inter (e.g., Figtree, DM Sans, or another warm/approachable option)
Defines spring config values with specific damping/stiffness numbers for the breathing animation
Uses semantic color tokens for dark mode (not hardcoded hex in components)
Does NOT use pure #000000 as dark mode background
Includes haptic feedback guidance for meaningful interactions (timer completion, session selection)

evals.prompt: Build me a task management app for iOS and Android using React Native + Expo. I want it to feel super modern on iOS — like it belongs on iOS 26. Think Things 3 level of polish. Needs: task list with swipe actions, a quick-add sheet that slides up, drag to reorder tasks, and proper dark mode. Should feel snappy and precise, not bouncy.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend Liquid Glass for iOS 26 tab bar/headers with Android fallback, choose snappy spring personality, suggest platform-specific components (.ios/.android), include gesture handler patterns for swipe and drag, and produce code with iOS 26 materials awareness.
assertions: Announces it is using the react-native-mobile skill
Mentions iOS 26 Liquid Glass or @callstack/liquid-glass for modern iOS material
Provides Android fallback strategy (not just iOS-only implementation)
Chooses snappy animation personality with specific spring config values
Recommends Gesture Handler for swipe actions and drag-to-reorder
Includes platform-specific conventions (iOS sheets vs Android bottom sheets, or .ios/.android files)
Addresses dark mode with semantic tokens and near-black backgrounds
References Things 3 level of polish through specific implementation choices, not just aspirationally

evals.prompt: We're building a social fitness app — React Native, Expo. It has a feed of workout posts, a workout tracker with real-time animations, user profiles, and a leaderboard. The brand is energetic and bold — think Nike Run Club energy. We also have a marketing website that needs to share the same design language. I want the whole thing to feel premium and distinctive. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: Should route to frontend-design for the full aesthetic treatment (6-axis matrix, creative seed, color system) since there's a web companion and the user wants serious design work. Should define bouncy/energetic spring personality. Should mention coordinating design tokens across mobile and web.
assertions: Announces it is using the react-native-mobile skill
Routes to or mentions frontend-design for full design direction (6-axis matrix, creative seed) since user wants premium + has web companion
Mentions coordinating design tokens between mobile app and marketing website
Chooses bouncy or energetic animation personality (not smooth or snappy) matching 'Nike Run Club energy'
Recommends FlashList for the workout feed
Suggests bold, non-blue primary color appropriate for an energetic fitness brand
Addresses the real-time animation needs for the workout tracker with Reanimated
